operation: Start by creating a workplane offset 16 units below the default XY plane, sketch a circle with radius 31, then extrude this sketch 170 units upwards to form the main cylindrical body. Next, select the bottom (lower Z-axis) face of this main cylinder, create a workplane aligned to this face, sketch a circle with radius 23, extrude this sketch 5 units downwards. Finally, select the new bottom face of the just extruded smaller cylinder, create a workplane on this face, sketch a regular 6-sided hexagon with a 19 unit diameter, extrude this sketch 12 units downwards to finish the model.
geometry: The model is a vertically oriented, fully axisymmetric structure consisting of three coaxially aligned segments stacked sequentially from top to bottom: a tall, large-diameter cylinder making up the majority of the upper structure, a shorter smaller-diameter cylindrical segment directly below it, and a short regular hexagonal prism as the lowest end component, with all three segments sharing a single central vertical axis.
function: This is a stepped mechanical shaft with a hexagonal drive interface at its lower end, designed for use as a power transmission component, fastener, or similar machine part that requires torque to be applied via the hexagonal feature.
